steps.title: Isolate the exponential
steps.content:
Move $b$ to the other side and divide by $7$.
$$(\frac{a}{4})^{x+c}=\frac{b}{7}$$

steps.title: Count the solutions
steps.content: Because $a>4$, the base $\frac{a}{4}$ is greater than $1$. A positive-base exponential function takes each positive value exactly once, and $\frac{b}{7}$ is positive. The answer is B.
